+using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
+using System.ComponentModel;
+
+namespace McpHttpServer.Resources;
+
+[McpServerResourceType]
+public sealed class ConfigResource
+{
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Exposes application configuration as an MCP resource.
+    /// The URI template uses a "section" parameter so clients can
+    /// request specific configuration sections by name.
+    /// </summary>
+    [McpServerResource(
+        UriTemplate = "config://app/{section}",
+        Name = "Application Configuration",
+        Description = "Returns configuration data for the requested section.",
+        MimeType = "application/json")]
+    public string GetConfig(string section, IConfiguration configuration)
+    {
+        var data = new Dictionary<string, string?>();
+        configuration.GetSection(section).Bind(data);
+
+        if (data.Count == 0)
+            return System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(
+                new { error = $"Section '{section}' not found or empty." });
+
+        return System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(data);
+    }
+}
+

            for m in matches:
                print(m)
                print('---')
            break

Let me fix the resource to use `[Description]` as a separate attribute on the method:
